deviceLab.title: Operate the 5008 for online-HDF
deviceLab.subtitle: Rotate and inspect the machine, dialyzer, and substitution path — and see what the 5008 platform delivers that a legacy 4008S cannot.
deviceLab.legacyNote: High-flux HD only. Online-HDF needs a 5008S/6008-class platform — a 4008S cannot deliver it. (All specs IFU-pending.)
evidence.title: Why high-volume HDF — the honest trial arc
evidence.subtitle: Set a convection dose and watch the modeled outcome move — then see the real landmark trials, including the null ones. The benefit is dose-dependent, which is exactly why it's credible.
evidence.eduNote: Educational model — modeled indices, not clinical prescription. Trial values cited.
evidence.arcTitle: The landmark trials — null and positive
evidence.arcNote: Shown in full: the null trials didn't fail HDF — they didn't reach high convection volume. The benefit is dose-dependent, appearing above ~23 L.
admin.title: Supervisor dashboard

admin.heatmapTitle: Where the unit is weak — competency × knowledge-type
admin.weakestAction: target remediation here; this is the cohort's gap.

idh.caseSubtitle: Diabetic, 3×/week in-center HD via AVF. Large interdialytic weight gain (+3.8 kg); UF goal 3.5 L over 4 h. Took his morning antihypertensive. 90 min in he reports lightheadedness, yawning, and a left calf cramp.
idh.eduDisclaimer: Educational model — not clinical decision support. Monitor values IFU-pending.
idh.option.a: Stop/reduce UF + Trendelenburg + small saline bolus
idh.option.b: Large 500 mL+ hypertonic/saline bolus, continue UF
idh.consequence.a: SBP recovers to 112/70 by t=100; the cramp eases. Stop/reduce ultrafiltration, lay the patient flat, give a measured saline bolus — but avoid excess fluid replacement (sodium overload).
idh.consequence.b: Transient rise then rebound; the session ends sodium-overloaded and sets up next-session hypertension and thirst. Excess fluid replacement should be avoided.
idh.consequence.c: t=95 SBP 78/44, near-syncope, session aborted. IDH is not benign — it carries myocardial stunning and hypoperfusion risk.
idh.selfReferenced: Self-referenced — your growth, not a leaderboard.
idh.debrief.heading: Debrief
idh.debrief.body: IDH is multifactorial — plasma-refill vs UF rate, autonomic dysfunction, cardiac reserve, thermal balance. The win is the prevention bundle + modality, not heroic rescue. Outcome tie-in: CONVINCE showed lower IDH incidence with high-volume HDF vs high-flux HD — the device story and the patient story are the same story.

deviceLab.system.title: System
deviceLab.system.subtitle: Switch generation — note which supports online-HDF.
deviceLab.dialyzer.title: Dialyzer
deviceLab.conv.title: Convective clearance
deviceLab.conv.hdfNote: Online-HDF adds convection to remove middle molecules diffusion alone misses.
deviceLab.conv.hdNote: HD only — diffusive clearance, no convective component.
deviceLab.config.title: This configuration
deviceLab.prime.title: Pre-treatment Prime Check
deviceLab.prime.subtitle: Before starting an online-HDF session on the 5008S, confirm which of the following items must be verified. Select Yes or No for each.
deviceLab.prime.eduNote: Educational model — not clinical decision support. Device values IFU-pending.
deviceLab.prime.item.diasafe.label: DIASAFE®plus ultrafilter installed on substitution outlet
deviceLab.prime.item.diasafe.explain: The DIASAFE®plus ultrafilter is mandatory for online-HDF — it sterilises substitution fluid to pharmaceutical-grade. Without it, online HDF is contraindicated. (IFU-pending)
deviceLab.prime.item.onlineLine.label: Online substitution-fluid line connected to AutoSub port
deviceLab.prime.item.onlineLine.explain: The substitution line routes ultrapure fluid post-dialyser for post-dilution HDF. Confirming the line is in place is a pre-treatment safety check. (IFU-pending)
deviceLab.prime.item.rinseback.label: Rinseback saline bag connected as substitution source
deviceLab.prime.item.rinseback.explain: Rinseback saline is used for end-of-treatment blood return — not for substitution fluid. Online-HDF substitution must come from the machine's own ultrafiltered water pathway, not an external bag.
deviceLab.prescribe.title: Prescribe Convective Volume
deviceLab.prescribe.subtitle: High-volume HDF requires ≥23 L convective volume per session to deliver the outcome benefit seen in the CONVINCE trial. Use the controls below to build the prescription. (Educational approximation — values IFU-pending.)
deviceLab.prescribe.passedNote: ≥23 L reached — high-volume HDF prescription gate passed.
deviceLab.prescribe.neededNote: {n} L still needed to reach the ≥23 L high-volume threshold.
deviceLab.prescribe.stepNote: Each step represents one substitution bolus increment (educational approximation; not a clinical dose calculator).
deviceLab.alarms.title: TMP Alarm Scenario
deviceLab.alarms.body: During an online-HDF session on the 5008S, a Transmembrane Pressure (TMP) High alarm sounds. The alarm is audible and the TMP reading has climbed above the set limit within the last 2 minutes. The patient is stable. What is your first response?
deviceLab.alarms.eduNote: Educational scenario — clinical protocols may vary by site. IFU-pending.
deviceLab.alarms.opt.checkKink.label: Inspect the venous return line for kinks or occlusion and clear
deviceLab.alarms.opt.checkKink.conseq: Correct. A kinked venous line is the most common mechanical cause of acute TMP rise. Clearing the occlusion restores venous outflow, drops TMP, and allows treatment to continue safely.
deviceLab.alarms.opt.increaseQb.label: Increase blood flow (QB) to overcome the pressure
deviceLab.alarms.opt.increaseQb.conseq: Incorrect. Increasing QB against an occlusion raises TMP further and risks clotting the dialyser or rupturing the venous line. The mechanical cause must be resolved first.
deviceLab.alarms.opt.ignore.label: Silence the alarm and continue — TMP alarms are often transient
deviceLab.alarms.opt.ignore.conseq: Incorrect. TMP alarms should never be silenced without investigation. An unresolved TMP rise can progress to haemofilter clotting, circuit loss, or (if a membrane rupture is present) patient harm.
deviceLab.alarms.opt.stop.label: Stop treatment immediately and call the nephrologist
deviceLab.alarms.opt.stop.conseq: Partially correct as an escalation step, but premature without first inspecting for a simple mechanical cause. A brief systematic check (line, clamps, patient arm position) usually resolves the alarm without interrupting treatment.
deviceLab.signoff.title: Device Lab Sign-off
deviceLab.signoff.body: You have completed the guided Device Lab sequence:
deviceLab.signoff.item1: Explored the 5008S CorDiax 3D model and compared it to the legacy 4008S
deviceLab.signoff.item2: Confirmed the DIASAFE®plus ultrafilter and online substitution-fluid line requirements
deviceLab.signoff.item3: Reached the ≥23 L convective volume prescription threshold
deviceLab.signoff.item4: Responded correctly to a TMP High alarm scenario
deviceLab.signoff.confirm: I confirm that I have reviewed this educational module as an HCP in a learning context. I understand this is not clinical guidance and all device values are IFU-pending.
deviceLab.signoff.mastered: Device Lab mastered — C3 gate complete
deviceLab.mastered.banner: Device Lab mastered — C3 gate recorded. You can revisit any section using the controls above.
